contents:
# Rubio joint statement accuses China of detaining Panama ships over canal dispute

## Overview
US Secretary Rubio warned China on Panama Canal sovereignty in joint statement with Latin allies. Accused Beijing of detaining Panama-flagged vessels to pressure over port control. Framed as infringement on hemisphere sovereignty tied to broader US-China tensions.

## Details
Statement issued with Bolivia, Costa Rica, Guyana, Paraguay, Trinidad and Tobago. Rubio declared "the sovereignty of our hemisphere remains non-negotiable." US regulators tracked nearly 70 detentions since March 8, 2026. China expert Gordon Chang called it part of Beijing's proxy leverage pattern. Detentions linked to Panama's canal port decisions amid US influence. Reuters reported surge as retaliation tactic.[1]

**Location**: Washington, United States

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @ChineseEmbinUS, which covers Chinese Embassy US diplomatic statements Taiwan arms sales. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Chinese Embassy US diplomatic statements Taiwan arms sales will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
